data.title: Trump recounts details of the dinner security incident, stating he does not believe the shooting was related to the conflict with Iran.
data.description: In response to reporters' questions, US President Trump said the incident at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner that day was "completely shocking." He heard a noise but thought it was a tray falling to the ground. "I was looking around," he said, but his wife was quicker to get out of the way and seemed to realize what had happened. Trump said he wanted to continue the dinner and promised to reschedule it as soon as possible. Trump stated that he had not received any briefings about threats before the dinner. Trump also mentioned previous assassination attempts against him and called on the American people to "reconcile their differences." He also noted that people from both parties condemned the incident that evening. Trump also thanked the media, saying they "acted very responsibly in their reporting." When asked if the shooting was related to the conflict with Iran, Trump said: "I don't think so." (Jin Shi)

data.title: LAB's price surged over 20% in the last 24 hours, with a suspected team address transferring another 18 million LAB tokens to a Bitget deposit address yesterday.
data.description: According to Arkham monitoring, the core-related address for LAB (0x1e8e...E166) transferred tokens to the Bitget deposit address (0x6593...E0bF) again yesterday. Statistics show that this address received a total of 18 million LAB tokens in two transactions on April 25th, with a total value of approximately $15.39 million. Currently, the circulating supply of LAB is 230.4M, and the total amount received by this Bitget deposit address represents approximately 7.81% of its total circulating supply. LAB is currently trading at $0.8560, a 24-hour increase of 18.82%, with a circulating market capitalization of approximately $197 million. The large-scale accumulation of tokens on exchanges during the price surge may indicate subsequent selling for cash or market makers adjusting their positions. Previous reports suggested that an address suspected to be from the LAB team transferred 100 million tokens to two Bitget deposit addresses, representing 43.4% of the total circulating supply.

data.title: Hong Kong Police Uncover Cross-Border Phone Scam Targeting Students
data.description: Hong Kong police have revealed the dismantling of a cross-border phone scam targeting mainland students studying in the UK and Australia. According to ChainCatcher, the criminal group impersonated mainland officials, deceiving victims by claiming their involvement in money laundering and other criminal activities. Victims were instructed to 'cooperate with investigations' by traveling to Hong Kong and purchasing gold nuggets from jewelry stores as 'security deposits.' Each purchase was kept under HKD 120,000 to avoid registration requirements, with the group collecting and transferring the stolen goods. The gold was then melted into bars or exchanged for cash and virtual currency. Authorities have received seven related reports, with total losses amounting to approximately HKD 7 million, and the highest single case loss reaching HKD 1.6 million.

data.title: Trump released close-up photos of the attackers, saying the shooting was unexpected.
data.description: US President Trump addressed the White House press briefing room, saying the shooting was truly unexpected. He also posted close-up photos of the attacker at the White House correspondents' dinner on social media. (Jinshi)

data.title: Hong Kong police have busted a money laundering scam involving virtual currency "gold nuggets," with approximately HK$7 million involved.
data.description: Hong Kong police have disclosed the cracking of a cross-border telephone fraud case. A criminal syndicate impersonating mainland officials specifically targeted mainland students studying in the UK and Australia. The scammers falsely claimed victims were involved in money laundering or other criminal cases, demanding their "cooperation with the investigation." The victims were then instructed to travel to Hong Kong and purchase gold bars from jewelry stores multiple times as "deposit," with each purchase not exceeding HK$120,000 to circumvent jewelry store registration regulations. The stolen gold was then collected and transferred by syndicate members. The gold bars were then melted down into gold bars or exchanged for cash and cryptocurrencies. Police have received reports of seven related cases, involving approximately HK$7 million, with the largest single loss reaching HK$1.6 million. (Hong Kong Wen Wei Po)

data.title: California Man Sentenced for Role in $263 Million Crypto Theft
data.description: A California resident has been sentenced to 70 months in prison for his involvement in a scheme that stole approximately $263 million in cryptocurrency assets. According to NS3.AI, the Department of Justice revealed that Evan Tangeman participated in social engineering fraud and home invasions to facilitate the theft. Court documents indicate that Tangeman pleaded guilty in December 2025, admitting to laundering at least $3.5 million in illicit funds.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Addresses Unexpected Shooting Incident
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump delivered a speech at the White House press briefing room, expressing surprise over a recent shooting incident. According to Odaily, Trump also shared a close-up photo of the assailant from the White House Correspondents' Dinner on social media.

data.title: Bitcoin's Potential Rebound Sparks Debate Amid Regulatory Developments
data.description: Paul Atkins's speech on April 21 regarding "Project Crypto" has reignited discussions about Bitcoin's ability to surpass the $80,000 mark, following its recent trading near $77,586 on Saturday, April 25. According to NS3.AI, the focus of the debate centers on a signed memorandum of understanding between the SEC and CFTC, alongside a proposed "innovation exemption" for on-chain trading of tokenized securities. However, Scott Melker has cautioned that the rebound might still turn into a bull trap.

data.title: The U.S. Department of Justice sentenced members of a $263 million cryptocurrency fraud ring to 70 months in prison for alleged social engineering fraud and money laundering involving luxury spending.
data.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) announced that Evan Tangeman, a 22-year-old California man, has been sentenced to 70 months (approximately 5 years and 10 months) in prison, with an additional 3-year supervised release period, for his involvement in a criminal organization that stole approximately $263 million in crypto assets through social engineering fraud and burglary. According to the indictment, Tangeman pleaded guilty in December 2025 to assisting the criminal network in laundering at least $3.5 million in illicit funds. The group is accused of using the stolen money for luxury spending, including multi-million dollar nightclub bills, Lamborghini sports cars, and Rolex watches. U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro of the District of Columbia stated in a press release that the organization “built its criminal system on an almost absurd level of greed,” emphasizing that Tangeman not only participated in money laundering but also destroyed evidence after his accomplices were arrested, demonstrating a clear criminal intent. This sentencing comes as data shows that the crypto industry lost $482 million in the first quarter of 2026 due to fraud and hacking, with social engineering fraud and offline violent robberies on the rise. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: SEC Chair Paul Atkins Advances 'Project Crypto' with CFTC Collaboration
data.description:
U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chair Paul Atkins has reaffirmed the advancement of 'Project Crypto' and announced collaboration with the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) to establish a digital asset classification framework. According to Odaily, this framework aims to clarify when tokens are considered securities and introduces an 'innovation exemption' to support on-chain trading of tokenized securities.

Market observers view Atkins' initiatives as one of the most proactive shifts in crypto regulation in SEC history, signaling a departure from the previous enforcement-based approach to clear rule-making. This move may encourage institutional capital to enter the market, potentially driving Bitcoin prices above $80,000. Currently, Bitcoin is priced at approximately $77,586, with the market closely watching Atkins' upcoming statements at the Bitcoin 2026 conference in late April.

data.title: Forbes: Expectations for BTC to return to $80,000 are rising; SEC Chairman's remarks at the Bitcoin 2026 conference draw attention.
data.description: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chairman Paul Atkins recently reiterated his commitment to "Project Crypto" and announced a collaboration with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) to develop a digital asset classification framework, clarifying when tokens are classified as securities. He also announced an "innovation exemption" to support on-chain trading of tokenized securities. The market believes that Atkins's initiatives represent one of the most proactive shifts in SEC regulation in history, marking a formal abandonment of the old "enforcement over regulation" model in favor of clear rule-making. This move could signal stronger entry for long-term institutional investors and potentially push Bitcoin's price back above $80,000. Currently, Bitcoin is priced at approximately $77,586, and the market is closely watching Atkins's further statements at the Bitcoin 2026 conference in late April. (Forbes)

data.title: Trump: Gunman involved in White House Correspondents' Dinner has been arrested
data.description: US President Trump said on social media that one of the gunmen involved in the "security incident" at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner has been arrested. Trump also stated that law enforcement has evacuated the area according to regulations, and this will be carried out immediately. He added that he will hold a press conference in the White House Press Briefing Room in 30 minutes. The First Lady, the Vice President, and all Cabinet members are in good health. He has spoken with all those responsible for the event, and we will reschedule the event within 30 days. (Jin Shi)

data.title: U.S. President Trump Announces Rescheduling of Event After Security Incident
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that law enforcement has requested his departure from a location due to procedural requirements. According to Foresight News, Trump stated that he will hold a press conference in the White House briefing room in 30 minutes. He assured that the First Lady, Vice President, and all Cabinet members are safe. Trump also mentioned that he has communicated with the event organizers and the event will be rescheduled within 30 days.

data.title: Trump Advocates for Crypto Regulation Amid Banking Concerns
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump emphasized the importance of advancing the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act during a private event at Mar-a-Lago in Florida. According to Foresight News, Trump stated that the White House will not allow banking lobbyists to hinder the progress of this legislation, which aims to establish a regulatory framework for cryptocurrencies and stablecoins. He highlighted the mainstream acceptance of the crypto industry and asserted that the United States is a leader in the crypto space.

The event, described as one of the world's most exclusive conferences, was attended by hundreds of major TRUMP meme coin holders. Notable guests included Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino, Ark Invest founder Cathie Wood, Anchorage Digital CEO Nathan McCauley, and boxing legend Mike Tyson. Previously, U.S. banks expressed concerns that stablecoin reward mechanisms could disrupt traditional deposit businesses, which had temporarily slowed the legislative process.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Announces Arrest Following White House Event Incident
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced on social media that a gunman involved in a 'security incident' at the White House Correspondents' Dinner has been apprehended. According to Odaily, Trump stated that law enforcement has ordered an evacuation of the premises, which will be carried out immediately. He also mentioned that a press conference will be held in the White House briefing room in 30 minutes. The First Lady, Vice President, and all Cabinet members are reported to be in good health. Trump has communicated with all event organizers and plans to reschedule the event within 30 days.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Safe After Security Incident at White House Correspondents' Dinner
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump was confirmed to be safe following a security incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. According to NS3.AI, the event will proceed as planned despite the occurrence. Further details on the nature of the incident have not been disclosed.

data.title: Aave Launches DeFi United Rescue Plan Amid rsETH Incident
data.description:
Aave has initiated a rescue plan called DeFi United in response to the rsETH incident. According to ChainCatcher, the initiative is led by Aave founder Stani Kulechov and seeks community donations in ETH through public fundraising.

Recent data reveals that DeFi United has successfully raised over 100,000 ETH, with more than 85,000 addresses participating.

data.title: Aave has launched a DeFi United rescue plan, raising over 100,000 ETH.
data.description: In response to the rsETH incident on April 18th, Aave launched a rescue plan called DeFi United, spearheaded by Aave founder Stani Kulechov. This plan called for community donations of ETH through a public fundraising campaign. Latest data shows that DeFi United has raised over 100,000 ETH, worth over $232 million, with over 85,000 addresses participating.

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description:
1. A shooting occurred at the White House Correspondents' Dinner; the gunman died, but Trump is "safe and sound."
2. Iranian Deputy Speaker: Mojtaba ordered that the Strait of Hormuz must not return to its pre-war state.
3. Aave founder: Sufficient recovery funds have been raised to fully fill the rsETH deficit.
4. The US military stated that it will continue to impose a complete blockade on ships entering and leaving Iranian ports.
5. An entity transferred 34.61 million ASTER tokens to Aster in the early morning, worth $22.94 million.
6. Trump: Cryptocurrency is a "huge industry."
7. Litecoin disclosed a zero-day vulnerability that triggered a DoS attack and abnormal MWEB transactions; the vulnerability was fixed after a block reorganization.
8. Polymarket's probability of a "permanent peace agreement between the US and Iran before April 30" is only 4%, and the cryptocurrency fell 7% in 24 hours.
9. Balancer attackers have exchanged 21,000 ETH for 617.43 BTC in the past three days.
10. RookieXBT's associated wallet spent 25,000 USDC to buy 1.89 million SPIKE tokens.

data.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner Set to Resume with Trump Expected to Attend
data.description: The White House Correspondents' Dinner is anticipated to resume soon, with further details to be shared shortly. According to Foresight News, the event's host has confirmed that U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to return to the event and deliver a speech.
